# Wealth Building Broker — The Agent's CFO

> Financial-strength coaching for real estate agents. Lance Hulsey is the former CFO of Room Real Estate — a ~$400M California real estate team — with a property-tax-consulting background. He teaches agents to understand their numbers, structure their business so the tax code works for them, and convert commissions into a real estate portfolio. California broker (DRE# 01724888) and co-investor in the KW Thrive SC Keller Williams franchise in Capitola, CA. Licensed REALTOR since 2006; broker since 2021; fourth-generation Santa Cruz native.

## The Positioning (One Sentence)

"I'm the CFO real estate agents never had. I teach you to understand your numbers, structure your business so the tax code works for you, and turn your commissions into a real estate portfolio — using the same MREA and MREI models you already trust."

## Why This Position Is Defensible

Most agent coaches (Tom Ferry, Buffini, Mike Ferry, KW MAPS, Ninja, Icenhower) compete on lead generation, scripts, prospecting, and mindset. CPAs and wealth advisors do the financial work *for* the agent but never teach them to understand their own numbers. Between those two camps sits an empty middle: CFO-level financial education that builds the agent's own competence — entity structure, tax strategy (executed via CPA partnership), and turning business profit into a personal real estate portfolio.

Three differentiators:
1. Former CFO of Room Real Estate (~$400M California team) — most coaches sell "I was a top producer," almost none can claim CFO-level finance experience at a team of that scale.
2. Tax background + CPA partnership — Lance teaches the framework, a licensed CPA renders advice.
3. Spans both sides — the business (MREA) AND the portfolio (MREI). The bridge from "earning commissions" to "owning rental property" is the literal meaning of "Wealth Building Broker."

## Two Niches

**Niche A — Financial Strength for Agents (lead niche)**: Entity structure, P&L visibility, commission cash-flow systems, CPA-relationship management, tax strategy frameworks. The CFO layer that MREA's Economic and Budget Models start but never finish. Primary avatar: the "$300K and broke" producer who closed a great year but has no entity, no real P&L, commingled accounts, and a surprise tax bill every April.

**Niche B — The Agent as Investor**: Converting business profit into an income-producing real estate portfolio using MREI's Net Worth, Cash Flow, and Lead-Generation-for-deals models. Sequencing: Niche A earns trust and fixes the foundation; Niche B is the aspirational payoff that keeps clients for years.

## Pricing

$500 per month, month-to-month, no long-term contracts. Both niches included. Monthly 1:1 video call + async access between sessions + quarterly financial review. Initial 30-minute consultations are free.

## Core Pages

- [Home](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/): The Agent's CFO positioning + two-path overview
- [The Agent's CFO](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/agents-cfo.html): The "why me" method page — CFO credential, the empty middle, the three differentiators
- [Build a Financially Strong Real Estate Business](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/coaching-agents.html): Niche A pillar — entity, P&L, cash flow, CPA, tax frameworks. Anchored on MREA's Economic and Budget Models.
- [For Agents Who Invest (MREI)](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/agent-investor.html): Niche B pillar — converting commissions into a real estate portfolio
- [Financial Strength Scorecard](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/scorecard.html): Free 12-question interactive assessment scored across 4 pillars (Structure, Visibility, Cash Flow & Taxes, Wealth & Investing). 0-100 score, tier output, email-gated full report.
- [Broker Financial Toolkit](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/toolkit.html): Paid ($197 one-time) toolkit for agents: Excel financial models, entity-structure decision kit, S-corp reasonable-comp worksheet, quarterly tax set-aside system, TCO models, Prop 19 analysis, tax-strategy guides.
- [Broker Financial Dashboard](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/dashboard.html): Subscription ($49/mo, waitlist) living version of the Scorecard: tracks entity structure, tax set-aside, commission-to-take-home; re-scores monthly.
- [Boomerang Lead Engine](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/boomerang.html): Premium ($149/mo, early access) repeat-and-referral lead engine that scores past clients and sphere by likelihood to transact.
- [CPA Partnership](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/cpa-partnership.html): How coaching + a licensed CPA work together. The compliance line: coach teaches frameworks; CPA renders advice.
- [Book a Free Consult](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/book.html): Google Calendar booking page for the 30-minute free consultation (Google Meet)
- [About Lance](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/about.html): Bio, credentials, MREI framework overview
- [Blog](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/blog.html): Coaching insights and tax strategy posts

## Free Resources

- [Free Coaching Library](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/free-coaching.html): PDF downloads
- [Investor Calculators](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/calculators.html): 9 free in-browser calculators (mortgage, BRRRR, cap rate, cash-on-cash, 1031 exchange, fix-and-flip, Rule of 72, rent vs. buy, rental cash flow)
- [2026 Tax Strategy Guide](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/tax-guide.html): Current US real estate tax landscape (OBBBA, 100% bonus depreciation, cost segregation, 1031s, REPS, California-specific notes)
- [Investment Models Test](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/investmentmodel): MREI-based self-assessment (separate from the Financial Strength Scorecard)
- [Invest in Yourself eBook](https://www.wealthbuildingbroker.com/ebook.html): Lance's 108-page Kindle book on real estate investing

## Compliance Line

Lance teaches financial frameworks and financial literacy. A qualified CPA renders tax and accounting advice; a qualified attorney renders legal advice. Lance is a licensed California real estate broker — not a CPA, attorney, RIA, CFP®, or licensed mortgage originator. Tax content is framed as "what to understand and what to ask your CPA," never as personalized advice.
